title: Deep Partial Type

statement.markdown:
## Problem Statement
Create a way to make a type partially deep, allowing consumers to selectively omit nested properties.

constraints: Typescript version should be ^4.6.2 or later.
You should create a generic DeepPartial type that can be applied to any type.
The type should handle nested objects correctly

starterCode.typescript:
file: deep-partial.ts
content: type DeepPartial<T> = T | Partial<T>

gradingCriteria.description: The solution compiles without any errors.
The DeepPartial type allows for selective omission of nested properties.
The solution handles edge cases (e.g. arrays of objects) correctly.

judgeConfig.referenceSolution:
file: deep-partial.ts
content: type DeepPartial<T> = {
        [P in keyof T]?: T[P] extends (infer U)[]
          ? DeepPartial<U>[] 
          : T[P] extends object
            ? DeepPartial<T[P]> 
            : T[P]
      }
